text:
An operator moving through a defile or canyon or on a mountain highway shall:

(1) hold the vehicle under control and as near the right-hand edge of the highway as possible; and

(2) on approaching a curve that obstructs the view of the highway for 200 feet, give warning with the horn of the motor vehicle.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 545. Operation & Movement of Vehicles
##### § 545.405 — Driving on Mountain Highway

An operator moving through a defile or canyon or on a mountain highway shall:

    (1) hold the vehicle under control and as near the right-hand edge of the highway as possible; and

    (2) on approaching a curve that obstructs the view of the highway for 200 feet, give warning with the horn of the motor vehicle.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.text: An operator moving through a defile or canyon or on a mountain highway shall:
hold the vehicle under control and as near the right-hand edge of the highway as possible; and
on approaching a curve that obstructs the view of the highway for 200 feet, give warning with the horn of the motor vehicle.
